unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* for HTTP proxy tunnel stuff */
/* for the week day and month names */
/* required for Curl_sockaddr_storage */
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */
/* macro to check for a three-digit ftp status code at the start of the given string */
/* macro to check for the last line in an FTP server response */
/* Local API functions */
unit.function_decl: static void _ftp_state(struct Curl_easy *data, ftpstate newstate);
static CURLcode ftp_quit(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);
static CURLcode ftp_parse_url_path(struct Curl_easy *data);
static CURLcode ftp_regular_transfer(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);
static void ftp_pasv_verbose(struct Curl_easy *data, struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, char *newhost, /* ascii version */ int port);
static CURLcode ftp_state_prepare_transfer(struct Curl_easy *data);
static CURLcode ftp_state_mdtm(struct Curl_easy *data);
static CURLcode ftp_state_quote(struct Curl_easy *data, bool init, ftpstate instate);
static int ftp_need_type(struct connectdata *conn, bool ascii);
static CURLcode ftp_do(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);
static CURLcode ftp_done(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLcode, bool premature);
static CURLcode ftp_connect(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);
static CURLcode ftp_disconnect(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, bool dead_connection);
static CURLcode ftp_do_more(struct Curl_easy *data, int *completed);
static CURLcode ftp_multi_statemach(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *done);
static int ftp_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);
static int ftp_domore_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);
static CURLcode ftp_doing(struct Curl_easy *data, bool *dophase_done);
static CURLcode ftp_setup_connection(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);
static CURLcode init_wc_data(struct Curl_easy *data);
static CURLcode wc_statemach(struct Curl_easy *data);
static void wc_data_dtor(void *ptr);
static CURLcode ftp_state_retr(struct Curl_easy *data, curl_off_t filesize);
static CURLcode ftp_readresp(struct Curl_easy *data, int sockindex, struct pingpong *pp, int *ftpcode, size_t *size);
static CURLcode ftp_dophase_done(struct Curl_easy *data, bool connected);
unit.decl_stmt: const struct Curl_handler Curl_handler_ftp = { "FTP", /* scheme */ ftp_setup_connection, /* setup_connection */ ftp_do, /* do_it */ ftp_done, /* done */ ftp_do_more, /* do_more */ ftp_connect, /* connect_it */ ftp_multi_statemach, /* connecting */ ftp_doing, /* doing */ ftp_getsock, /* proto_getsock */ ftp_getsock, /* doing_getsock */ ftp_domore_getsock, /* domore_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* perform_getsock */ ftp_disconnect, /* disconnect */ ZERO_NULL, /* write_resp */ ZERO_NULL, /* connection_check */ ZERO_NULL, /* attach connection */ PORT_FTP, /* defport */ CURLPROTO_FTP, /* protocol */ CURLPROTO_FTP, /* family */ PROTOPT_DUAL | PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION | PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD | PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY | PROTOPT_PROXY_AS_HTTP | PROTOPT_WILDCARD /* flags */ };

const struct Curl_handler Curl_handler_ftps = { "FTPS", /* scheme */ ftp_setup_connection, /* setup_connection */ ftp_do, /* do_it */ ftp_done, /* done */ ftp_do_more, /* do_more */ ftp_connect, /* connect_it */ ftp_multi_statemach, /* connecting */ ftp_doing, /* doing */ ftp_getsock, /* proto_getsock */ ftp_getsock, /* doing_getsock */ ftp_domore_getsock, /* domore_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* perform_getsock */ ftp_disconnect, /* disconnect */ ZERO_NULL, /* write_resp */ ZERO_NULL, /* connection_check */ ZERO_NULL, /* attach connection */ PORT_FTPS, /* defport */ CURLPROTO_FTPS, /* protocol */ CURLPROTO_FTP, /* family */ PROTOPT_SSL | PROTOPT_DUAL | PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION | PROTOPT_NEEDSPWD | PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY | PROTOPT_WILDCARD /* flags */ };

function.name: close_secondarysocket

unit.comment: /* * NOTE: back in the old days, we added code in the FTP code that made NOBODY * requests on files respond with headers passed to the client/stdout that * looked like HTTP ones. * * This approach is not very elegant, it causes confusion and is error-prone. * It is subject for removal at the next (or at least a future) soname bump. * Until then you can test the effects of the removal by undefining the * following define named CURL_FTP_HTTPSTYLE_HEAD. */

function.name: freedirs

unit.comment:
/*********************************************************************** * * Lineend Conversions * On ASCII transfers, e.g. directory listings, we might get lines * ending in '
' and we prefer just '
'. * We might also get a lonely '' which we convert into a '
'. */

function.name: ftp_cw_lc_write

unit.comment: /* CURL_DO_LINEEND_CONV */

/*********************************************************************** * * AcceptServerConnect() * * After connection request is received from the server this function is * called to accept the connection and close the listening socket * */

function.name: AcceptServerConnect

unit.comment: /* * ftp_timeleft_accept() returns the amount of milliseconds left allowed for * waiting server to connect. If the value is negative, the timeout time has * already elapsed. * * The start time is stored in progress.t_acceptdata - as set with * Curl_pgrsTime(..., TIMER_STARTACCEPT); * */

function.name: ftp_timeleft_accept

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ReceivedServerConnect() * * After allowing server to connect to us from data port, this function * checks both data connection for connection establishment and ctrl * connection for a negative response regarding a failure in connecting * */

function.name: ReceivedServerConnect

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * InitiateTransfer() * * After connection from server is accepted this function is called to * setup transfer parameters and initiate the data transfer. * */

function.name: InitiateTransfer

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * AllowServerConnect() * * When we've issue the PORT command, we have told the server to connect to * us. This function checks whether data connection is established if so it is * accepted. * */

function.name: AllowServerConnect
ftp_endofresp
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, char *line, size_t len, int *code)

function.name: ftp_readresp
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, int sockindex, struct pingpong *pp, int *ftpcode, /* return the ftp-code if done */ size_t *size)

unit.comment: /* --- parse FTP server responses --- */
/* * Curl_GetFTPResponse() is a BLOCKING function to read the full response * from a server after a command. * */

function.name: Curl_GetFTPResponse
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, ssize_t *nreadp, /* return number of bytes read */ int *ftpcode)

unit.comment: /* for debug purposes */
/* This is the ONLY way to change FTP state! */

function.name: _ftp_state
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, ftpstate newstate #ifdef DEBUGBUILD , int lineno #endif )

function.name: ftp_state_user
ftp_state_pwd

unit.comment: /* For the FTP "protocol connect" and "doing" phases only */

function.name: ftp_getsock

unit.comment: /* For the FTP "DO_MORE" phase only */

function.name: ftp_domore_getsock

unit.comment:
/* This is called after the FTP_QUOTE state is passed.
ftp_state_cwd() sends the range of CWD commands to the server to change to the correct directory. It may also need to send MKD commands to create missing ones, if that option is enabled. */

function.name: ftp_state_cwd
ftp_state_use_port
ftp_state_use_pasv

unit.comment: /* * ftp_state_prepare_transfer() starts PORT, PASV or PRET etc. * * REST is the last command in the chain of commands when a "head"-like * request is made. Thus, if an actual transfer is to be made this is where we * take off for real. */

function.name: ftp_state_prepare_transfer
ftp_state_rest
ftp_state_size
ftp_state_list
ftp_state_retr_prequote
ftp_state_stor_prequote
ftp_state_type

unit.comment: /* This is called after the CWD commands have been done in the beginning of the DO phase */

function.name: ftp_state_mdtm

unit.comment: /* This is called after the TYPE and possible quote commands have been sent */

function.name: ftp_state_ul_setup
ftp_state_quote

unit.comment: /* called from ftp_state_pasv_resp to switch to PASV in case of EPSV problems */

function.name: ftp_epsv_disable
control_address
match_pasv_6nums
ftp_state_pasv_resp
ftp_state_port_resp
ftp_213_date
function.parameter_list: (const char *p, int *year, int *month, int *day, int *hour, int *minute, int *second)

function.name: client_write_header
ftp_state_mdtm_resp
ftp_state_type_resp
ftp_state_retr
ftp_state_size_resp
ftp_state_rest_resp
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int ftpcode, ftpstate instate)

function.name: ftp_state_stor_resp

unit.comment: /* for LIST and RETR responses */

function.name: ftp_state_get_resp

unit.comment: /* after USER, PASS and ACCT */

function.name: ftp_state_loggedin

unit.comment: /* for USER and PASS responses */

function.name: ftp_state_user_resp

unit.comment: /* for ACCT response */

function.name: ftp_state_acct_resp
ftp_statemachine

unit.comment: /* called repeatedly until done from multi.c */

function.name: ftp_multi_statemach
ftp_block_statemach

unit.comment: /* * ftp_connect() should do everything that is to be considered a part of * the connection phase. * * The variable 'done' points to will be TRUE if the protocol-layer connect * phase is done when this function returns, or FALSE if not. * */

function.name: ftp_connect

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_done() * * The DONE function. This does what needs to be done after a single DO has * performed. * * Input argument is already checked for validity. */

function.name: ftp_done

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_sendquote() * * Where a 'quote' means a list of custom commands to send to the server. * The quote list is passed as an argument. * * BLOCKING */

function.name: ftp_sendquote

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_need_type() * * Returns TRUE if we in the current situation should send TYPE */

function.name: ftp_need_type

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_nb_type() * * Set TYPE. We only deal with ASCII or BINARY so this function * sets one of them. * If the transfer type is not sent, simulate on OK response in newstate */

function.name: ftp_nb_type
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, bool ascii, ftpstate newstate)

unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * * ftp_pasv_verbose() * * This function only outputs some informationals about this second connection * when we've issued a PASV command before and thus we have connected to a * possibly new IP address. * */

function.name: ftp_pasv_verbose
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct Curl_addrinfo *ai, char *newhost, /* ascii version */ int port)

unit.comment: /* * ftp_do_more() * * This function shall be called when the second FTP (data) connection is * connected. * * 'complete' can return 0 for incomplete, 1 for done and -1 for go back * (which basically is only for when PASV is being sent to retry a failed * EPSV). */

function.name: ftp_do_more

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_perform() * * This is the actual DO function for FTP. Get a file/directory according to * the options previously setup. */

function.name: ftp_perform
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, bool *connected, /* connect status after PASV / PORT */ bool *dophase_done)

function.name: wc_data_dtor
init_wc_data
wc_statemach

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_do() * * This function is registered as 'curl_do' function. It decodes the path * parts etc as a wrapper to the actual DO function (ftp_perform). * * The input argument is already checked for validity. */

function.name: ftp_do

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_quit() * * This should be called before calling sclose() on an ftp control connection * (not data connections). We should then wait for the response from the * server before returning. The calling code should then try to close the * connection. * */

function.name: ftp_quit

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_disconnect() * * Disconnect from an FTP server. Cleanup protocol-specific per-connection * resources. BLOCKING. */

function.name: ftp_disconnect

unit.comment: /* warning C4706: assignment within conditional expression */

function.name: ftp_parse_url_path

unit.comment: /* call this when the DO phase has completed */

function.name: ftp_dophase_done

unit.comment: /* called from multi.c while DOing */

function.name: ftp_doing

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * ftp_regular_transfer() * * The input argument is already checked for validity. * * Performs all commands done before a regular transfer between a local and a * remote host. * * ftp->ctl_valid starts out as FALSE, and gets set to TRUE if we reach the * ftp_done() function without finding any major problem. */

function.name: ftp_regular_transfer
ftp_setup_connection

unit.comment: /* CURL_DISABLE_FTP */
